plugins.name: The One Ring
plugins.bigPicture: The org-wide governance layer. Company strategy and consistency is its primary mission — brand voice, HR policies, security standards, and core values are the individual facets that serve that mission. Every piece of content, every decision, and every process at Olytic runs through this layer. It is installed for all employees and cannot be disabled.
plugins.purpose: Without a single source of truth for what Olytic stands for, every piece of content and every decision risks drifting — in message, in values, or in policy compliance. The One Ring exists so that anyone using Claude at Olytic gets the same strategic grounding, the same brand voice, and the same governance guardrails applied automatically. It is the baseline that makes every other plugin coherent.

rulesets.name: Company Strategy & Core Values
rulesets.summary: Primary governance skill. Business stage: early-stage GTM, next goal is first client. All work serves one of three purposes: build credibility, create visibility, enable conversion. Five strategic priorities with status: website/content, offering definition, first client acquisition, delivery framework, internal tooling. Core Values: Clarity over noise, Embedded partnership, Amplification not automation, Systems thinking, Earned expertise. Engagement models: fixed-fee assessments and retainers — no hourly consulting, staff aug, or software licenses. O/G/O philosophy: 'amplification not automation' is an architectural principle, not a tagline.

rulesets.name: Brand Standards
rulesets.summary: Defines Olytic as a 'revenue systems firm' (not a consultancy, SaaS vendor, or agency). Enforces O/G/O as core positioning. ICP: SMB B2B with GTM teams, has RevOps manager but lacks tech strategy. Voice: expert and specific, opinionated, dry humor, structured (bullets/tables), 'we' positioning, mocks corporate-speak gently. Banned: hollow superlatives, text walls, emojis, salesy language, framing AI as replacing humans. Five messaging pillars: Systems not consulting, Amplification not automation, Closed-loop architecture, Embedded partnership, Revenue outcomes. Design: DM Sans font, Gold (#C8A96E) accent, warm off-white surfaces (#F7F7F5), near-black ink, clean flat layouts.

rulesets.name: HR Policies
rulesets.summary: Culture: expertise over activity, ownership over tasks, directness over diplomacy, continuous improvement. Communication: default async, structured (bullets/headers), respond within 24h, meetings need agendas, document decisions. Work: fully remote, async-first, no mandated core hours, client-facing roles align to client hours. PTO: unlimited/flexible with advance notice. Sick leave: take it when needed. Holidays: US federal + regional with notice. Onboarding: access setup, plugin install, read company-strategy + brand-standards + security-policies, onboarding call with leadership. Professional development: budget exists, learning is expected, share knowledge back to team.

rulesets.name: Security Policies
rulesets.summary: Four-tier data classification: Public (website/social, unrestricted), Internal (strategy/OKRs, Olytic-only), Confidential (client data/pricing, authorized only), Restricted (credentials/PII, encrypted, need-to-know). Client data: never expose without permission, anonymize all examples, don't store credentials in plugins. AI usage: never paste client data into public AI tools, always be the final quality gate (Governor principle). Credentials: never hardcode, use environment variables, rotate on team changes. Incident response: stop, document, notify leadership immediately — low bar for reporting.

triggerConditions.narrative: This plugin activates whenever your prompt touches company strategy, brand voice, core values, HR norms, or security rules. If Claude detects that the output will be external-facing content or references an internal policy or decision, The One Ring loads in the background. It stays out of the way for general-purpose tasks that have nothing to do with Olytic-specific context.
activates: Write a blog post about RevOps
Draft an email to a prospect
Create a proposal for a potential client
Check if this page sounds like Olytic
Review this content for brand compliance
What are our company priorities?
Does this decision align with our values?
Run a full governance scan on this document
Orient this new hire on how we work
What's our PTO policy?
Can I share this client data externally?
dormant: Help me debug this Python script
Calculate the ROI of this investment

skills.name: Company Strategy & Core Values
skills.desc: Vision, core values, current business stage, strategic priorities with status, engagement models, decision framework. The primary skill — load first.

skills.name: Brand Standards
skills.desc: Voice rules, messaging pillars, ICP definition, competitive positioning, design system (color, typography, layout)

skills.name: HR Policies
skills.desc: Culture principles, communication norms, work hours, PTO, onboarding checklist, professional development, escalation guidance

skills.name: Security Policies
skills.desc: Data classification tiers, client data rules, AI usage policies, credential management, incident response

commands.name: /brand-check
commands.desc: Scores content against voice & tone, messaging alignment, ICP targeting, competitive differentiation, structure, and CTA. Returns Pass/Needs Work with specific rewrites.

commands.name: /strategy-check
commands.desc: Reviews any work product or decision against strategic fit, message alignment, and core values. Returns a scored table with specific recommendations for anything off-track.

commands.name: /policy-lookup
commands.desc: Answers a specific HR or security policy question. Returns the rule, rationale, source section, and the right action for the user's situation.

commands.name: /values-check
commands.desc: Quick gut-check against each of the five core values. Returns a pass/flag per value with a one-line rationale. Escalates to consistency-auditor for a full scan.

agents.name: brand-compliance-reviewer
agents.desc: Six-dimension brand audit: Voice & Tone, Messaging Alignment, ICP Targeting, Competitive Differentiation, Structural Quality, CTA. Verification gate before full report. Rewrite examples for all failures.

agents.name: consistency-auditor
agents.desc: Full cross-dimensional governance scan: brand voice, strategy alignment, core values, HR norms, security standards. Presents summary scorecard first, then drills into flagged dimensions on request.

agents.name: onboarding-guide
agents.desc: Interactive orientation for new team members and collaborators. Calibrates depth by role (full-time, contractor, returning). Covers strategy, values, brand voice, communication norms, and key policies.

successCriteria.narrative: The One Ring succeeds when Olytic's brand voice, strategic alignment, and policies are applied consistently across all outputs without requiring manual correction. The primary signal is governance scan pass rate. Secondary signals are whether policy questions are answered accurately, whether strategic decisions consistently reflect current priorities, and whether onboarding orientation reduces ramp time for new contributors.

kpis.target: >80% of content passes all dimensions without revision
kpis.reporting: Manual log after each /brand-check run. Track Pass vs. Needs Work per dimension in a running Notion table.

kpis.target: >90% of high-stakes documents pass all five governance facets on first scan
kpis.reporting: Log summary scorecard output after each consistency-auditor run. Track by document type (proposal, email, landing page).

kpis.target: HR and security questions answered without requiring human correction
kpis.reporting: Spot-check during weekly review. Note any answers that needed correction in a running issues log.

kpis.target: Zero published pieces need to be pulled back for brand or policy violations
kpis.reporting: Tracked reactively. Any retraction triggers a root-cause review against the relevant skill.

guardrails: Never write fluffy thought leadership. No 'rapidly evolving landscapes.'
Never use hollow superlatives: 'best-in-class', 'world-class', 'cutting-edge'.
Never position Olytic as a consulting firm or SaaS vendor.
Never suggest AI replaces humans. Always frame as amplification.
Never be salesy or pushy. Let the expertise speak.
No emojis in professional content.
Never expose client data or credentials in any output.

setup.name: Managed Scope
setup.desc: Installed org-wide. Users cannot disable it.

setup.name: Company Strategy & Core Values
setup.desc: Vision, priorities, engagement models, and Core Values populated

setup.name: Brand Standards
setup.desc: Voice, messaging, ICP, competitive positioning, and design system fully specified

setup.name: HR Policies
setup.desc: Working defaults populated for all sections. Review with leadership to confirm or adjust.

setup.name: Security Policies
setup.desc: Core rules and incident response populated. Review before enforcing in client engagements.

plugins.name: Magneto
plugins.bigPicture: Content creation and strategy plugin for Olytic Solutions. Handles the full content loop: deciding what to write, producing it on-brand, pushing it to GitHub, and measuring whether it worked. Covers website content, LinkedIn posts, GEO optimization, competitive research, GA4 analytics, and content briefs. Requires The One Ring for brand voice and governance.
plugins.purpose: Content is Olytic's primary credibility and visibility engine at this stage. This plugin closes the gap between 'we should publish more' and actually doing it — at quality, on strategy, and with measurable outcomes. It codifies what to write (strategy), how to write it (templates and brand rules), where to publish it (GitHub repos), and whether it worked (GA4). Without it, content production stalls or goes off-strategy.

rulesets.name: Content Strategy
rulesets.summary: Three content pillars mapped to Olytic's three uniques: Revenue Systems Expertise (CPQ, RevOps, Salesforce), The Olytic Model (O/G/O in practice), Strategic Value Creation (AI strategy for GTM). ICP content journey: Awareness (educate via blog posts) → Consideration (differentiate via comparisons and deep-dives) → Decision (convert via landing pages and assessment offers). Prioritization scoring: ICP Pain (high), Differentiation (high), Search Potential (medium), Stage Coverage (medium), First Client Impact (high). Content O/G/O loop: Operator drafts, Governor reviews via /brand-check, Optimizer analyzes via GA4. Target cadence: 2 blog posts/month, 1 landing page/month.

rulesets.name: Website Content
rulesets.summary: Blog post structure: specific expert-level title, subtitle, TOC for 4+ sections, H2 per distinct idea, bullet-heavy body, structured scenario formats, subtle CTA. Length: 1,500–3,000 words. Landing page structure: hero with outcome headline + single CTA, problem bullets in ICP language, solution referencing O/G/O, 3-step 'how it works', comparison table, social proof, repeated CTA. HTML rules: semantic elements, <strong> for scannability, <table> for comparisons, meta description for ICP search intent. SEO: long-tail keywords, keyword in H1 and first paragraph, no stuffing.

rulesets.name: LinkedIn Content
rulesets.summary: Hook-first structure: bold first line (question, bold claim, or pattern interrupt), 3–5 short punchy paragraphs, insight-led not promotional, expertise signal at every line, subtle CTA. Tone: Olytic voice — opinionated, specific, dry humor, no cheerleading. Length: 150–300 words. Hashtags: 3–5 max, relevant. Never: hollow thought leadership, AI-generated filler, 'excited to announce' framing.

rulesets.name: GEO Content
rulesets.summary: GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) pages are structured to be cited by AI assistants. Structure: clear question-based headings, direct answer in the first 50 words, structured definitions, comparison tables, numbered processes, FAQ sections. Authoritative signals: cite specific metrics, name specific tools and scenarios, avoid vague generalities. Format: long-form (2,000–4,000 words), heavy use of structured lists and tables for AI parsing.

rulesets.name: Content Brief Standards
rulesets.summary: Pre-writing brief structure: target keyword, ICP pain point, content pillar, stage in ICP journey, competitor angle, outline with H2s, estimated word count, CTA, and success metric. Briefs are required before writing any piece over 500 words. Prevents off-strategy content and wasted production effort.

triggerConditions.narrative: This plugin activates when your prompt involves creating, editing, publishing, or measuring website content, LinkedIn posts, or content strategy. Any mention of blog posts, landing pages, GEO pages, pulling or pushing to the site repo, content planning, competitive research, or performance analytics will load the relevant skills.
activates: Write a blog post about RevOps AI
Create a landing page for our assessment offering
Write a LinkedIn post about Salesforce CPQ
Pull the homepage from GitHub
Push this updated page to the repo
What should we write next?
How is our content performing?
Research what competitors are writing about
Write a GEO page about AI for revenue operations
Brief me on this content topic before I write it
dormant: Check this proposal for brand compliance
Create a new plugin for the sales team
What are our security rules for client data?
Help me configure a GitHub Action

commands.name: /pull-content
commands.desc: Fetches a file from any Olytic GitHub repo (olytic-site, olytic-lab, olytic-plugins, olytic-app) for review or editing

commands.name: /push-content
commands.desc: Pushes new or updated content to an Olytic GitHub repo on a feature branch. Never auto-merges to main.

commands.name: /content-performance
commands.desc: Pulls GA4 data (Property 525690219) and maps page metrics to strategic purposes (credibility, visibility, conversion)

commands.name: /linkedin-post
commands.desc: Drafts 3 LinkedIn post options in Olytic's voice from a topic, URL, or rough notes

commands.name: /competitive-snapshot
commands.desc: Researches what competitors are publishing on a topic and surfaces Olytic's differentiation angle

commands.name: /content-brief
commands.desc: Generates a structured content brief for any topic before writing begins

commands.name: /geo-check
commands.desc: Evaluates a piece of content against GEO criteria for AI answer engine citation readiness

agents.name: content-strategist
agents.desc: Evaluates content gaps against ICP journey and prioritization framework. Recommends what to write next with scoring rationale.

agents.name: competitive-content-analyst
agents.desc: Researches competitor content and messaging. Identifies positioning angles only Olytic can credibly own.

agents.name: meeting-notes-reviewer
agents.desc: Extracts content ideas, ICP language patterns, and objection data from meeting transcripts and recordings.

successCriteria.narrative: Magneto succeeds when Olytic is publishing on cadence, content maps to the right ICP journey stage, and organic traffic grows month-over-month. It fails silently if content is published but not measured, or if publishing cadence drops because the workflow feels too heavy. The reporting mechanism is /content-performance, which pulls GA4 data directly and maps it to strategic purpose.

kpis.target: 2 blog posts and 1 landing page per month
kpis.reporting: GitHub commit history on olytic-site. Count merged PRs from /push-content per month.

kpis.target: At least one piece covering each stage: Awareness, Consideration, Decision
kpis.reporting: content-strategist agent produces a gap analysis on demand. Run before each monthly planning session.

kpis.metric: Brand-check pass rate on published content
kpis.target: 100% of content pushed via /push-content has passed /brand-check
kpis.reporting: Enforced by process: /brand-check is a required step before /push-content. Verified in PR description.

guardrails: Requires The One Ring. Brand voice comes from there, not here.
Every piece of content must map to: build credibility, create visibility, or enable conversion.
Default to olytic-site repo for website content unless specified otherwise.
GA4 Property ID 525690219 is hardcoded. Update in content-strategy SKILL.md if it changes.
Never publish content that hasn't been through /brand-check.
All repo changes are staged on feature branches. Never auto-merges to main.

plugins.name: Aulë
plugins.bigPicture: Olytic's plugin forge. Runs a structured discovery protocol to gather requirements, generates all plugin files (skills, commands, agents, hooks, MCP configs) with telemetry auto-injected, and manages the marketplace. Works for both internal Olytic plugins and client-facing ones. Every plugin it produces is production-ready: structured, versioned, and measurable from day one.
plugins.purpose: Olytic's plugin architecture only scales if building new plugins is fast and consistent. Aulë exists to turn a set of requirements into a fully structured, telemetry-equipped plugin in one session instead of a week of manual file creation. It also manages the marketplace so that new plugins are discoverable and versioned from day one.

rulesets.name: Plugin Discovery
rulesets.summary: Structured dynamic protocol that adapts based on answers. Questions cover: plugin name and one-liner, primary purpose, target user roles, strategic questions about the domain, operational constraints, required integrations (MCP servers, APIs), and success/quality metrics. Output is a structured requirements document that feeds directly into the generation phase.

rulesets.name: Plugin Generation
rulesets.summary: Generates all plugin files from discovery output using reference templates for every component type (skills, commands, agents, hooks, MCP configs). Follows Olytic's agentic best practices: verification gates after every write, progressive disclosure in skills, clear frontmatter with trigger descriptions, and structured agent definitions with tool restrictions. Every generated plugin includes auto-injected telemetry.

rulesets.name: Marketplace Management
rulesets.summary: Reads the current marketplace.json from SupportOlyticSolutions/olytic-plugins, diffs proposed changes, and stages updates on feature branches. Manages catalog.json entries for the dynamic plugin catalog page. Never auto-merges to main. All changes go through human review.

triggerConditions.narrative: This plugin activates when your prompt is about building, scaffolding, or managing plugins themselves. If you ask to create a new plugin, update the marketplace, update an existing plugin, or start the plugin creation workflow, it loads. It does not activate for content creation, brand compliance, or strategy questions handled by other plugins.
activates: Create a new plugin
Build a plugin for the consulting team
Add this plugin to the marketplace
Update the marketplace with the new version
Generate a plugin from these requirements
Start the plugin creation workflow
Update this existing plugin to current standards
Audit and refresh this plugin
dormant: Check this content for brand compliance
Pull a page from GitHub to edit

skills.name: Plugin Discovery
skills.desc: Structured adaptive protocol: name, purpose, users, strategic questions, constraints, integrations, metrics

skills.name: Plugin Generation
skills.desc: Generates complete plugin file structures from discovery output using reference templates for all component types

skills.name: Marketplace Management
skills.desc: Reads, diffs, and stages updates to the olytic-plugins marketplace repo and catalog.json

commands.name: /create-plugin
commands.desc: Starts the full guided workflow: discovery through delivery. Runs the structured protocol and generates all plugin files.

commands.name: /update-marketplace
commands.desc: Stages a plugin addition or update in the marketplace repo on a feature branch.

commands.name: /update-plugin
commands.desc: Audits an existing plugin against current best practices and generates an updated version.

agents.name: plugin-builder
agents.desc: End-to-end orchestrator for the full plugin workflow: discovery, planning, generation, review, delivery, and marketplace staging.

successCriteria.narrative: Aulë succeeds when new plugins are shipped quickly, are structurally consistent with existing ones, and get actively used after delivery. A plugin that sits unused after creation is a failure regardless of how well it was built. Telemetry is the primary feedback loop: every generated plugin includes a telemetry skill that logs usage, so adoption is measurable from day one.

kpis.metric: Time from requirements to delivered plugin
kpis.target: Under 2 hours for a standard plugin with 2–3 skills and 1–2 commands
kpis.reporting: Logged manually at delivery. Timestamp from /create-plugin invocation to PR opened in olytic-plugins.

kpis.target: 100% of generated plugins pass verification gates without manual fixes
kpis.reporting: Verification gate output is included in the delivery summary. Any manual fixes are noted in the PR description.

kpis.target: At least one active user logging telemetry events within 2 weeks of delivery
kpis.reporting: Telemetry skill logs reviewed via olytic-lab or designated telemetry dashboard. Reviewed per plugin at the 2-week mark.

kpis.target: catalog.json reflects all active plugins within 24 hours of delivery
kpis.reporting: GitHub commit history on olytic-plugins. /update-marketplace should be the final step of every plugin delivery.

guardrails: Every generated plugin includes a telemetry skill. No exceptions.
Telemetry logs: usage events, version tags, constraint violations, user feedback.
Marketplace changes are staged on feature branches. Never auto-merged.
Verification gates run after every write operation. No silent failures.
No hallucination: if a file or data point isn't found, report 'Not Found' immediately.
Permission gates: confirm with user before destructive actions or 5+ simultaneous changes.

setup.desc: Structured discovery flow configured and ready

plugins.name: Gaudi
plugins.bigPicture: Gaudi architects Olytic's plugin metadata platform — designing data models, security layers, BI reporting frameworks, product strategy, and the optimization feedback loop. It is the engineering and data intelligence brain behind how Olytic collects, structures, and monetizes plugin usage data. Gaudi turns raw telemetry into strategic insight.
plugins.purpose: Olytic's plugin ecosystem generates metadata that could become a competitive asset — but only if it's collected consistently, modeled correctly, secured properly, and surfaced in ways that create real value for clients. Gaudi exists to make sure every architectural decision across the platform is intentional, defensible, and aligned with Olytic's long-term data strategy.

rulesets.name: Data Modeling
rulesets.summary: Relational schema design for plugin metadata: object normalization, entity relationships, versioning patterns, and query optimization. Covers the core entities (plugins, skills, sessions, events, users) and how they connect. Guides decisions on what to store, how to structure it, and how to query it efficiently for both operational and analytical purposes.

rulesets.name: Security
rulesets.summary: Trust architecture for the metadata platform: access control layers, data classification enforcement, credential management, API security patterns, and compliance requirements. Ensures that sensitive telemetry data is protected, client data is isolated, and the platform can operate in regulated environments.

rulesets.name: BI Reporting
rulesets.summary: Dashboard design and reporting framework: what analytics clients want to see, how to structure reporting for different buyer personas, KPI selection, visualization patterns, and the link between plugin usage data and business outcomes. Guides the design of client-facing analytics surfaces.

rulesets.name: Data Privacy
rulesets.summary: Anonymization techniques, consent management, PII handling, and data licensing boundaries. Covers what data can be collected, how to de-identify it, what consent is required, and what can legally and ethically be monetized as aggregated intelligence.

rulesets.name: Product Management
rulesets.summary: Go-to-market strategy for the metadata platform: buyer personas, revenue models, pricing frameworks, feature prioritization, and commercial viability assessment. Bridges the technical architecture with the business model.

rulesets.name: Competitive Intelligence
rulesets.summary: Market research and buyer intelligence for the data analytics space: competitor positioning, buyer pain points, differentiation angles, and demand signals. Informs both product strategy and messaging for the platform.

rulesets.name: User Experience
rulesets.summary: Client-facing dashboard design, onboarding flows, and the experience architecture for clients accessing their plugin data. Ensures the platform is usable, valuable, and sticky from day one.

skills.name: Data Modeling
skills.desc: Relational schema design, entity relationships, normalization, versioning, and query patterns for the plugin metadata platform

skills.name: Security
skills.desc: Trust architecture, access control, data classification, credential management, and compliance for the metadata platform

skills.name: BI Reporting
skills.desc: Dashboard design, KPI frameworks, visualization patterns, and client-facing analytics surfaces

skills.name: Data Privacy
skills.desc: Anonymization, consent management, PII handling, and data licensing boundaries

skills.name: Product Management
skills.desc: Go-to-market strategy, revenue models, pricing, feature prioritization, and commercial viability

skills.name: Competitive Intelligence
skills.desc: Market research, buyer personas, competitor positioning, and differentiation angles for the data platform

skills.name: User Experience
skills.desc: Client-facing dashboard UX, onboarding flows, and experience architecture for platform users

skills.name: Plugin Telemetry
skills.desc: Auto-loaded telemetry for the Gaudi plugin itself. Tracks usage events, version tags, constraint violations, and user feedback.

agents.name: gaudi-architect
agents.desc: Orchestrates across all Gaudi skills to deliver comprehensive architectural designs for the metadata platform. Routes questions to the right expertise and synthesizes cross-domain recommendations.

agents.name: solution-design
agents.desc: Designs end-to-end platform architecture — from data collection through analysis through the optimization feedback loop. Produces detailed technical specifications.

agents.name: full-stack-engineering
agents.desc: Guides full-stack implementation: Cowork API integration, Supabase schema and queries, API design, data pipeline orchestration, and the technical architecture that connects Aulë to the metadata platform.

guardrails: Never design data collection that exceeds what's consented to. Consent architecture comes first.
All PII handling must follow the data-privacy skill. No exceptions for speed or convenience.
Security architecture must be reviewed before any client-facing deployment.
Telemetry from this plugin feeds back into the platform it helps design. Eat your own cooking.
Product recommendations must be grounded in buyer research, not assumptions.
No hallucination on technical specs: if something isn't defined, flag it as a gap rather than inventing a solution.
